017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.parquet;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.util.zip.*;
021
022/**
023 * Parquet compression codecs supported by this implementation.
024 *
025 * <p>
026 * UNCOMPRESSED and GZIP support both directions.  SNAPPY is <b>decode-only</b> (a dependency-free,
027 * pure-Java Snappy <i>block-format</i> decompressor) so Juneau can read spec-compliant Parquet from
028 * parquet-mr / Spark / Hive, which default to Snappy.  LZ4, Zstd, Brotli, and LZO require external
029 * libraries and remain unsupported (hard error on read).
030 */
031public enum CompressionCodec {
032
033   /** No compression. */
034   UNCOMPRESSED(0) {
035
036      @Override
037      byte[] compress(byte[] data) {
038         return data;
039      }
040
041      @Override
042      byte[] decompress(byte[] data, int uncompressedSize) {
043         return data;
044      }
045   },
046
047   /** Snappy block format — decode only (write is not supported). */
048   SNAPPY(1) {
049
050      @Override
051      byte[] compress(byte[] data) throws IOException {
052         throw new IOException("Snappy compression (write) is not supported; use UNCOMPRESSED or GZIP.");
053      }
054
055      @Override
056      byte[] decompress(byte[] data, int uncompressedSize) throws IOException {
057         return SnappyBlockDecompressor.decompress(data, uncompressedSize);
058      }
059   },
060
061   /** GZIP compression. */
062   GZIP(2) {
063
064      @Override
065      byte[] compress(byte[] data) throws IOException {
066         var baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();
067         try (var gzip = new GZIPOutputStream(baos)) {
068            gzip.write(data);
069         }
070         return baos.toByteArray();
071      }
072
073      @Override
074      byte[] decompress(byte[] data, int uncompressedSize) throws IOException {
075         var result = new byte[uncompressedSize];
076         try (var gzip = new GZIPInputStream(new ByteArrayInputStream(data))) {
077            int n = 0;
078            while (n < uncompressedSize) {
079               int r = gzip.read(result, n, uncompressedSize - n);
080               if (r <= 0)
081                  break;
082               n += r;
083            }
084            if (n < uncompressedSize)
085               throw new IOException("GZIP decompression produced fewer bytes than expected");
086         }
087         return result;
088      }
089   };
090
091   /** Thrift FileMetaData Codec enum value. */
092   final int thriftValue;
093
094   /**
095    * Constructor.
096    *
097    * @param thriftValue The Thrift codec value.
098    */
099   CompressionCodec(int thriftValue) {
100      this.thriftValue = thriftValue;
101   }
102
103   abstract byte[] compress(byte[] data) throws IOException;
104
105   abstract byte[] decompress(byte[] data, int uncompressedSize) throws IOException;
106
107   /**
108    * Resolves a Thrift {@code CompressionCodec} enum value to a supported codec.
109    *
110    * <p>
111    * {@code UNCOMPRESSED} (0) and {@code GZIP} (2) support both directions; {@code SNAPPY} (1) is
112    * decode-only.  Any other value — a known-but-unsupported codec (LZO/BROTLI/LZ4/ZSTD/LZ4_RAW) or an
113    * unrecognized id — is a hard error rather than a silent fallback to {@code UNCOMPRESSED}, which would
114    * otherwise treat still-compressed bytes as raw page data and surface silent garbage (GAP-5).
115    *
116    * @param value The Thrift codec enum value.
117    * @return The matching supported codec.
118    * @throws IOException If the codec is not supported by this implementation.
119    */
120   static CompressionCodec fromThrift(int value) throws IOException {
121      for (var c : values())
122         if (c.thriftValue == value)
123            return c;
124      throw new IOException("Unsupported Parquet compression codec: " + codecName(value)
125         + ". Only UNCOMPRESSED, GZIP, and SNAPPY (decode-only) are supported.");
126   }
127
128   private static String codecName(int value) {
129      return switch (value) {
130         case 3 -> "LZO (3)";
131         case 4 -> "BROTLI (4)";
132         case 5 -> "LZ4 (5)";
133         case 6 -> "ZSTD (6)";
134         case 7 -> "LZ4_RAW (7)";
135         default -> Integer.toString(value);
136      };
137   }
138}
